news.title: CMR Surgical kicks off Versius Plus robot’s US commercial launch
news.snippet: Following FDA approval last December for its upgraded robot, CMR Surgical has begun rolling out its Versius Plus platform in the U.S.

news.title: Philips AI cath lab copilot nets FDA clearance
news.snippet: Developed with Edwards Lifesciences, DeviceGuide is aimed at the challenging task of transcatheter mitral valve repair.

news.title: OrbiMed-backed Pinnacle adds $89M series B to power oral peptide pipeline
news.snippet: Oral peptide specialist Pinnacle Medicines has secured an $89 million series B with backing from investors in the U.S. and China, bringing the company’s total fundraising haul to $134 million.

news.title: Pulmatrix picked for reverse merger with anti-aging biotech weeks after Cullgen deal collapses
news.snippet: Pulmatrix may have been discarded by Cullgen, but the inhaled therapy biotech’s Nasdaq listing has caught the attention of another company.

news.title: Wave crashes after obesity trial tracks 1% dip in body weight over 6 months
news.snippet: Wave Life Sciences linked its obesity drug candidate to a 1% dip in body weight after six months, leading investors to drive the stock down by more than 50% despite the biotech hailing the data as pos

news.title: Biopharma funding dropped 20% in 2025 as companies doubled down on big bets: IQVIA
news.snippet: Biopharma funding fell 20% last year, reversing the post-pandemic recovery as the IPO haul dropped to the lowest level of the past 10 years, according to an IQVIA analysis.

news.title: Innate ends work on NK cell engager in phase 1/2 trial as part of ongoing pipeline refocus
news.snippet: Innate Pharma has finally called time on an NK cell engager in phase 1/2 development as part of an ongoing fine-tuning of the French biotech’s pipeline.

news.title: FDA approves Denali's Hunter syndrome drug, handing rare disease community a win
news.snippet: The FDA has approved Denali Therapeutics’ enzyme replacement therapy for a genetic lysosomal storage disease after a string of high-profile rejections for rare disease candidates.

news.title: After ph. 2 readout, analysts herald 'best-in-class potential' for Maze kidney candidate as stock plummets
news.snippet: Maze Therapeutics has revealed topline phase 2 data for its lead kidney disease candidate that has analysts heralding its “best-in-class potential.” But many investors were racing for the exits Wednes

news.title: Endogenex nets $50M to carry intestinal diabetes procedure to the FDA
news.snippet: The fundraising comes after clinical results showed its system met safety goals and led to metabolic improvements in HbA1c, fasting glucose levels and body weight.

news.title: Takeda targets $1.3B in cost savings in further restructuring
news.snippet: Takeda is setting the stage for a sweeping new round of restructuring, aiming to streamline operations to bankroll upcoming drug launches and late-stage pipeline development.

news.title: Merck, girding for Keytruda cliff, flies toward promising CML asset with $6.7B Terns acquisition
news.snippet: Merck and Terns Pharmaceuticals have agreed to a $6.7 billion all-cash buyout that delivers a prized leukemia asset to the New Jersey Big Pharma.

news.title: Sarepta's big bet on Arrowhead's siRNA assets yields early data
news.snippet: Sarepta Therapeutics has shared initial clinical data on two siRNA candidates, delivering an early look at the wisdom of its decision to bet big on Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals’ pipeline.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker '26: Gilgamesh's $60M series A; Immutrin raises $87M
news.snippet: The Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker records venture capital rounds of $50 million or more secured by biotechs.

news.title: Novo Nordisk prepares to go global as triple-G prospect passes midphase diabetes test in China
news.snippet: Novo Nordisk and its Chinese partner have reported another midphase win for their triple G candidate, sharing diabetes data to add to the momentum generated by an obesity readout. But while the data s

news.title: As questions swirl around ATTR competition, Alnylam plots path to market leadership for Amvuttra
news.snippet: Addressing pressing questions about the upcoming ATTR-CM competition and market opportunity, Alnylam management on Tuesday outlined a path for Amvuttra to become the revenue leader in an expansive ATT

news.title: Egg-based drugmaker Neion Bio emerges from stealth to cook up multi-product biosimilar collab
news.snippet: The new company, looking to eliminate costly manufacturing bottlenecks by producing biologic medicines in chicken eggs, has now lined up a commercial taker.

news.title: As expansions come online, CDMO Hovione aims to meet industry's 'dual supply and sourcing' zeal: exec
news.snippet: With key expansions coming to fruition on either side of the pond, specialist CDMO Hovione is making sure that drugmakers can access its particle engineering expertise across multiple geographies.

news.title: Genentech walks the walk in lupus as sponsor of annual awareness and fundraising event
news.snippet: Roche’s Genentech is backing this year's Walk to End Lupus Now, taking up a sponsorship opportunity claimed by GSK in previous years.

news.title: Outspoken ACIP member steps down amid vaccine panel uncertainty: reports
news.snippet: After becoming a prominent public face of the CDC’s revamped Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices, Robert Malone, M.D., has reportedly stepped down from his role, citing mounting drama in rece

news.title: How pharma marketers can capitalize on HCPs’ AI, social media and streaming habits
news.snippet: A new report from Assembled Intelligence and inVibe sheds light on how HCPs are using digital technologies in their work—and what that means for marketers.

news.title: Corcept's lead drug bounces back from FDA snub with different approval as Lifyorli in ovarian cancer
news.snippet: The FDA has given the all-clear to Corcept’s class-first Lifyorli (relacorilant) to treat adults with platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer after one to th

news.title: Ionis slashes Tryngolza's price tag by 93% ahead of anticipated label expansion
news.snippet: The company is lowering the annual list price of Tryngolza from $595,000 to $40,000 ahead of an anticipated expansion into the much larger market of severe hypertriglyceridemia.

news.title: FDA approves Denali's Hunter syndrome drug, handing rare disease community a win
news.snippet: The FDA has approved Denali Therapeutics’ enzyme replacement therapy for a genetic lysosomal storage disease after a string of high-profile rejections for rare disease candidates.

news.title: Novartis sued by breast cancer patient over branded drug websites’ data-sharing practices
news.snippet: Novartis is facing a class-action lawsuit alleging it shared, without patients’ consent, sensitive health information it collected from visitors to its consumer-facing branded drug websites.

news.title: As Boehringer touts US launches, board chairman worries EU is 'falling further behind'
news.snippet: While touting the uptake of new drugs Hernexeos and Jascayd in the U.S., Boehringer Ingelheim's chairman leveled criticism of regulatory red tape in Europe, where the treatments have yet to be approve

news.title: Biogen pays $20M upfront to tap into Alteogen's subQ delivery tech
news.snippet: Biogen will hand Alteogen $20 million upfront for help developing subcutaneous formulations of two unnamed biologics using Alteogen’s delivery technology.

news.title: Takeda targets $1.3B in cost savings in further restructuring
news.snippet: Takeda is setting the stage for a sweeping new round of restructuring, aiming to streamline operations to bankroll upcoming drug launches and late-stage pipeline development.

news.title: Regulatory tracker: CSL adds febrile seizure warning to Flucelvax label at FDA's request
news.snippet: In this tracker, Fierce Pharma is recording the regulatory progress of in-market products, including expansions into key geographies and new indications. Some of these updates may not meet the bar for

news.title: #FierceMadness: ‘Z’s reign supreme to reach Sweet 16 in 2026 Drug Name Tournament—VOTE NOW
news.snippet: It’s time once again for Fierce Pharma Marketing’s annual #FierceMadness tournament. This time around, we're watching 64 newly minted drug names go head to head.

news.title: STAT+: FDA approves Rocket gene therapy for rare immune disorder
news.snippet: Kresladi has been tested in just nine patients but has had positive results.

news.title: Eli Lilly’s deal man, Allogene’s off-the-shelf CAR-T, and Merck’s Terns buy
news.snippet: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: Eli Lilly's deal man, Allogene's off-the-shelf CAR-T, and Merck's Terns buy.

news.title: STAT+: Denali Therapeutics, Corcept Therapeutics win FDA approvals
news.snippet: In today's Readout Newsletter, Denali and Corcept win FDA approvals, Sarepta eyes a rebound, and Allogene progresses with an off-the-shelf CAR-T.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about an FDA OK for a rare disease drug, a grilling of the PTO chief, and more
news.snippet: The FDA approved a Denali medicine for Hunter syndrome, a notable decision as the agency has recently taken tougher stances on rare disease drugs

news.title: STAT+: Allogene’s off-the-shelf CAR-T is nearing an early but pivotal study readout
news.snippet: A preliminary readout from a study could vindicate Allogene Therapeutics' stubborn belief in its off-the-shelf CAR-T treatment.

news.title: California considers seal of approval for foods that are not ultra-processed
news.snippet: A California bill would allow "not ultraprocessed" labels on foods, even as scientists debate definitions and courts question the legality of similar laws.

news.title: Hospital waiting lists in Wales see record drop
news.snippet: Wait times for planned care are falling but diagnostic and cancers waits are up, latest figures indicate.
